1→"""Profile extraction module for Boss直聘.
     2→
     3→Extracts detailed candidate profile information from Boss直聘.
     4→Profile details are shown as a floating overlay (浮层) on the search page,
     5→not as a separate page. The extraction JS also supports standalone profile
     6→pages as a fallback.
     7→"""
     8→
     9→from typing import Optional, Dict, Any
    10→
    11→from loguru import logger
    12→from rich.console import Console
    13→from rich.panel import Panel
    14→
    15→from src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
    16→from src.utils.delays import async_random_delay
    17→from src.utils.rate_limiter import get_rate_limiter, async_check_rate_limit
    18→from src.utils.captcha import check_captcha
    19→from src.utils.retry import retry_navigation
    20→from src.models.database import Candidate, get_db_manager
    21→from src.exceptions.errors import ExtractionError
    22→
    23→console = Console()
    24→
    25→
    26→# JavaScript extraction function for Boss直聘 profile pages
    27→EXTRACT_PROFILE_JS = """
    28→() => {
    29→    const result = {
    30→        name: '',
    31→        title: '',
    32→        company: '',
    33→        location: '',
    34→        salary_expect: '',
    35→        work_years: '',
    36→        education_level: '',
    37→        basic_info: {},
    38→        education: [],
    39→        work_experience: [],
    40→        skills: []
    41→    };
    42→
    43→    // Extract basic info from the profile header area
    44→    const nameEl = document.querySelector(
    45→        '[class*="geek-name"], [class*="name"], h1[class*="name"], .name'
    46→    );
    47→    result.name = nameEl ? nameEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    48→
    49→    const titleEl = document.querySelector(
    50→        '[class*="expect-position"], [class*="title"], [class*="position"]'
    51→    );
    52→    result.title = titleEl ? titleEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    53→
    54→    const companyEl = document.querySelector(
    55→        '[class*="company-name"], [class*="company"]'
    56→    );
    57→    result.company = companyEl ? companyEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    58→
    59→    const locationEl = document.querySelector(
    60→        '[class*="expect-area"], [class*="location"], [class*="city"]'
    61→    );
    62→    result.location = locationEl ? locationEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    63→
    64→    const salaryEl = document.querySelector(
    65→        '[class*="expect-salary"], [class*="salary"]'
    66→    );
    67→    result.salary_expect = salaryEl ? salaryEl.innerText.trim() : '';
    68→
    69→    // Extract info tags (work years, education, age, etc.)
    70→    const tagEls = document.querySelectorAll(
    71→        '[class*="info-tag"], [class*="base-info"] span, [class*="geek-tags"] span, ' +
    72→        '[class*="tag-item"], [class*="basic-info"] span'
    73→    );
    74→    const tags = Array.from(tagEls).map(el => el.innerText.trim()).filter(t => t);
    75→
    76→    for (const tag of tags) {
    77→        if (tag.includes('年') && (tag.includes('经验') || /^\\d/.test(tag))) {
    78→            result.work_years = tag;
    79→            result.basic_info.work_years = tag;
    80→        } else if (['博士', '硕士', '本科', '大专', '中专', '高中'].some(d => tag.includes(d))) {
    81→            result.education_level = tag;
    82→            result.basic_info.education = tag;
    83→        } else if (tag.includes('岁')) {
    84→            result.basic_info.age = tag;
    85→        } else if (tag.includes('求职') || tag.includes('状态')) {
    86→            result.basic_info.job_status = tag;
    87→        }
    88→    }
    89→
    90→    // Extract education details
    91→    const eduSection = document.querySelector(
    92→        '[class*="resume-education"], [class*="education-list"], [class*="edu-experience"]'
    93→    );
    94→    if (eduSection) {
    95→        const eduItems = eduSection.querySelectorAll(
    96→            '[class*="resume-item"], [class*="edu-item"], [class*="item"], li'
    97→        );
    98→        for (const item of eduItems) {
    99→            const text = item.innerText.trim();
   100→            if (!text) continue;
   101→            const lines = text.split('\\n').filter(l => l.trim());
   102→            const edu = {};
   103→            if (lines.length >= 1) edu.school = lines[0].trim();
   104→            if (lines.length >= 2) edu.degree = lines[1].trim();
   105→            if (lines.length >= 3) edu.major = lines[2].trim();
   106→            if (lines.length >= 4) edu.period = lines[3].trim();
   107→            result.education.push(edu);
   108→        }
   109→    }
   110→
   111→    // Extract work experience
   112→    const workSection = document.querySelector(
   113→        '[class*="resume-work"], [class*="work-list"], [class*="work-experience"]'
   114→    );
   115→    if (workSection) {
   116→        const workItems = workSection.querySelectorAll(
   117→            '[class*="resume-item"], [class*="work-item"], [class*="item"], li'
   118→        );
   119→        for (const item of workItems) {
   120→            const text = item.innerText.trim();
   121→            if (!text) continue;
   122→            const lines = text.split('\\n').filter(l => l.trim());
   123→            const work = {};
   124→            if (lines.length >= 1) work.company = lines[0].trim();
   125→            if (lines.length >= 2) work.title = lines[1].trim();
   126→            if (lines.length >= 3) work.period = lines[2].trim();
   127→            if (lines.length >= 4) work.description = lines.slice(3).join(' ').trim();
   128→            result.work_experience.push(work);
   129→        }
   130→    }
   131→
   132→    // Extract project experience (Boss直聘 specific)
   133→    const projectSection = document.querySelector(
   134→        '[class*="resume-project"], [class*="project-list"]'
   135→    );
   136→    if (projectSection) {
   137→        result.basic_info.has_project = true;
   138→    }
   139→
   140→    // Extract skills
   141→    const skillEls = document.querySelectorAll(
   142→        '[class*="skill-tag"], [class*="skill-item"], [class*="tag-item"], ' +
   143→        '[class*="skill"] span'
   144→    );
   145→    const skills = new Set();
   146→    for (const el of skillEls) {
   147→        const text = el.innerText.trim();
   148→        if (text && text.length < 30) {
   149→            skills.add(text);
   150→        }
   151→    }
   152→    result.skills = Array.from(skills);
   153→
   154→    // Extract advantages/self description
   155→    const advantageEl = document.querySelector(
   156→        '[class*="resume-advantage"], [class*="self-desc"], [class*="geek-advantage"]'
   157→    );
   158→    if (advantageEl) {
   159→        result.basic_info.self_description = advantageEl.innerText.trim();
   160→    }
   161→
   162→    return result;
   163→}
   164→"""
   165→
   166→
   167→async def extract_profile(
   168→    boss_id: str,
   169→    browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None,
   170→) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   171→    """
   172→    Extract detailed profile for a candidate.
   173→
   174→    Args:
   175→        boss_id: Boss直聘 candidate ID (e.g., "zp_xxx")
   176→        browser: Browser manager instance
   177→
   178→    Returns:
   179→        Dictionary with profile data
   180→
   181→    Raises:
   182→        ExtractionError: If profile extraction fails
   183→    """
   184→    if browser is None:
   185→        browser = get_browser_manager()
   186→
   187→    page = browser.page
   188→    if not page:
   189→        page = await browser.start()
   190→
   191→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   192→    db_manager = get_db_manager()
   193→
   194→    # Rate limit check
   195→    await async_check_rate_limit('view_profile')
   196→
   197→    # Boss直聘 shows profile as a floating overlay (浮层) on the search page.
   198→    # Strategy 1: Try to extract from an already-open overlay on the current page.
   199→    # Strategy 2: Navigate to profile URL as a fallback.
   200→    encrypt_id = boss_id.replace('zp_', '') if boss_id.startswith('zp_') else boss_id
   201→
   202→    # First check if we have a profile URL in the database
   203→    profile_url = None
   204→    with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   205→        candidate = session.query(Candidate).filter_by(boss_id=boss_id).first()
   206→        if candidate and candidate.profile_url:
   207→            profile_url = candidate.profile_url
   208→
   209→    if not profile_url:
   210→        profile_url = f"https://www.zhipin.com/web/geek/{encrypt_id}"
   211→
   212→    try:
   213→        # Strategy 1: Check if a profile overlay is already open on the current page
   214→        overlay_present = await page.evaluate("""
   215→            () => {
   216→                const overlay = document.querySelector(
   217→                    '[class*="dialog-wrap"].active, [class*="boss-popup__wrapper"],
   218→                     [class*="geek-detail"], [class*="resume-dialog"], [class*="profile-panel"]'
   219→                );
   220→                return overlay !== null;
   221→            }
   222→        """)
   223→
   224→        if not overlay_present:
   225→            # Strategy 2: Navigate to profile page
   226→            await retry_navigation(page, profile_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   227→            await async_random_delay('page_load')
   228→
   229→            # Check for captcha
   230→            await check_captcha(page, report_to_rate_limiter=True)
   231→
   232→            # Wait for profile content to load
   233→            await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
   234→
   235→        # Extract profile data via JavaScript (works on both overlay and standalone page)
   236→        profile_data = await page.evaluate(EXTRACT_PROFILE_JS)
   237→
   238→        if not profile_data or not profile_data.get('name'):
   239→            logger.warning(f"Could not extract profile for {boss_id}")
   240→            raise ExtractionError(f"Failed to extract profile data for {boss_id}")
   241→
   242→        # Log action
   243→        rate_limiter.log_action('view_profile', {'boss_id': boss_id})
   244→
   245→        # Update database
   246→        _update_profile_in_db(boss_id, profile_data, db_manager)
   247→
   248→        # Display profile
   249→        _display_profile(boss_id, profile_data)
   250→
   251→        return profile_data
   252→
   253→    except ExtractionError:
   254→        raise
   255→    except Exception as e:
   256→        logger.error(f"Error extracting profile for {boss_id}: {e}")
   257→        raise ExtractionError(f"Failed to extract profile for {boss_id}: {e}")
   258→
   259→
   260→def _update_profile_in_db(boss_id: str, profile_data: Dict, db_manager) -> None:
   261→    """Update candidate profile in the database."""
   262→    with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   263→        candidate = session.query(Candidate).filter_by(boss_id=boss_id).first()
   264→
   265→        if not candidate:
   266→            # Create new candidate entry
   267→            candidate = Candidate(boss_id=boss_id)
   268→            session.add(candidate)
   269→
   270→        # Update fields
   271→        candidate.name = profile_data.get('name') or candidate.name
   272→        candidate.title = profile_data.get('title') or candidate.title
   273→        candidate.company = profile_data.get('company') or candidate.company
   274→        candidate.location = profile_data.get('location') or candidate.location
   275→        candidate.salary_expect = profile_data.get('salary_expect') or candidate.salary_expect
   276→        candidate.work_years = profile_data.get('work_years') or candidate.work_years
   277→        candidate.education_level = profile_data.get('education_level') or candidate.education_level
   278→        candidate.basic_info = profile_data.get('basic_info')
   279→        candidate.education = profile_data.get('education')
   280→        candidate.work_experience = profile_data.get('work_experience')
   281→        candidate.skills = profile_data.get('skills')
   282→
   283→
   284→def _display_profile(boss_id: str, profile_data: Dict) -> None:
   285→    """Display extracted profile in Rich format."""
   286→    name = profile_data.get('name', 'Unknown')
   287→    title = profile_data.get('title', '')
   288→    company = profile_data.get('company', '')
   289→    location = profile_data.get('location', '')
   290→    salary = profile_data.get('salary_expect', '')
   291→    work_years = profile_data.get('work_years', '')
   292→    education_level = profile_data.get('education_level', '')
   293→
   294→    # Header
   295→    header = f"[bold]{name}[/bold]"
   296→    if title:
   297→        header += f" - {title}"
   298→    if company:
   299→        header += f" @ {company}"
   300→
   301→    console.print(Panel(header, title=f"Profile: {boss_id}", border_style="green"))
   302→
   303→    # Basic info
   304→    info_parts = []
   305→    if location:
   306→        info_parts.append(f"Location: {location}")
   307→    if salary:
   308→        info_parts.append(f"Expected Salary: {salary}")
   309→    if work_years:
   310→        info_parts.append(f"Experience: {work_years}")
   311→    if education_level:
   312→        info_parts.append(f"Education: {education_level}")
   313→
   314→    if info_parts:
   315→        console.print("  " + " | ".join(info_parts))
   316→
   317→    # Education
   318→    education = profile_data.get('education', [])
   319→    if education:
   320→        console.print("\n  [cyan]Education:[/cyan]")
   321→        for edu in education:
   322→            parts = []
   323→            if edu.get('school'):
   324→                parts.append(edu['school'])
   325→            if edu.get('degree'):
   326→                parts.append(edu['degree'])
   327→            if edu.get('major'):
   328→                parts.append(edu['major'])
   329→            console.print(f"    - {' | '.join(parts)}")
   330→
   331→    # Work experience
   332→    work_exp = profile_data.get('work_experience', [])
   333→    if work_exp:
   334→        console.print("\n  [cyan]Work Experience:[/cyan]")
   335→        for work in work_exp:
   336→            line = ""
   337→            if work.get('company'):
   338→                line += work['company']
   339→            if work.get('title'):
   340→                line += f" - {work['title']}"
   341→            if work.get('period'):
   342→                line += f" ({work['period']})"
   343→            console.print(f"    - {line}")
   344→
   345→    # Skills
   346→    skills = profile_data.get('skills', [])
   347→    if skills:
   348→        console.print(f"\n  [cyan]Skills:[/cyan] {', '.join(skills)}")
   349→
   350→    # Self description
   351→    basic_info = profile_data.get('basic_info', {})
   352→    if basic_info.get('self_description'):
   353→        desc = basic_info['self_description'][:200]
   354→        console.print(f"\n  [cyan]Self Description:[/cyan]\n    {desc}")
   355→
   356→    console.print()
   357→

1→"""Chrome process lifecycle manager for Web service.
     2→
     3→Manages starting, health-checking, and restarting the Chrome browser
     4→process with CDP enabled. Used by the Web service lifespan to ensure
     5→Chrome is always available for browser automation tasks.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import asyncio
     9→import shutil
    10→import signal
    11→import subprocess
    12→import sys
    13→from pathlib import Path
    14→from typing import Optional
    15→
    16→import httpx
    17→from loguru import logger
    18→
    19→from src.utils.config import get_config
    20→
    21→
    22→class ChromeProcessManager:
    23→    """Manages the Chrome browser process for the Web service.
    24→
    25→    - Auto-starts Chrome in headed mode with --remote-debugging-port
    26→    - Periodically health-checks via CDP HTTP endpoint
    27→    - Auto-restarts Chrome if it crashes
    28→    - Gracefully terminates Chrome on shutdown
    29→    """
    30→
    31→    def __init__(self):
    32→        self._config = get_config()
    33→        self._process: Optional[subprocess.Popen] = None
    34→        self._health_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None
    35→        self._running = False
    36→
    37→    @property
    38→    def debug_port(self) -> int:
    39→        return self._config.get('web.chrome_debug_port', 9222)
    40→
    41→    @property
    42→    def user_data_dir(self) -> str:
    43→        return self._config.get('web.chrome_user_data_dir', 'data/.chrome-profile')
    44→
    45→    @property
    46→    def cdp_url(self) -> str:
    47→        return f'http://127.0.0.1:{self.debug_port}'
    48→
    49→    @property
    50→    def is_chrome_running(self) -> bool:
    51→        return self._process is not None and self._process.poll() is None
    52→
    53→    def _find_chrome_binary(self) -> str:
    54→        """Find Chrome binary on the current platform."""
    55→        if sys.platform == 'darwin':
    56→            candidates = [
    57→                '/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome',
    58→                '/Applications/Chromium.app/Contents/MacOS/Chromium',
    59→            ]
    60→        elif sys.platform == 'win32':
    61→            candidates = [
    62→                r'C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe',
    63→                r'C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe',
    64→            ]
    65→        else:
    66→            candidates = [
    67→                'google-chrome',
    68→                'google-chrome-stable',
    69→                'chromium',
    70→                'chromium-browser',
    71→            ]
    72→
    73→        for candidate in candidates:
    74→            if Path(candidate).exists() or shutil.which(candidate):
    75→                return candidate
    76→
    77→        raise RuntimeError(
    78→            'Chrome binary not found. Install Google Chrome or set the path manually.'
    79→        )
    80→
    81→    async def _check_cdp_health(self) -> bool:
    82→        """Check if Chrome CDP endpoint is responsive."""
    83→        try:
    84→            async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
    85→                resp = await client.get(
    86→                    f'{self.cdp_url}/json/version',
    87→                    timeout=5.0,
    88→                )
    89→                return resp.status_code == 200
    90→        except Exception:
    91→            return False
    92→
    93→    def _start_chrome_process(self) -> None:
    94→        """Start Chrome with CDP enabled."""
    95→        chrome_binary = self._find_chrome_binary()
    96→        user_data = Path(self.user_data_dir).resolve()
    97→        user_data.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    98→
    99→        args = [
   100→            chrome_binary,
   101→            f'--remote-debugging-port={self.debug_port}',
   102→            f'--user-data-dir={user_data}',
   103→            '--no-first-run',
   104→            '--no-default-browser-check',
   105→            '--disable-background-timer-throttling',
   106→            '--disable-backgrounding-occluded-windows',
   107→            '--disable-renderer-backgrounding',
   108→        ]
   109→
   110→        logger.info(f'Starting Chrome: {" ".join(args[:3])}...')
   111→        self._process = subprocess.Popen(
   112→            args,
   113→            stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   114→            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   115→        )
   116→        logger.info(f'Chrome started with PID {self._process.pid}')
   117→
   118→    async def start(self) -> None:
   119→        """Start Chrome and begin health monitoring.
   120→
   121→        If Chrome is already running on the debug port (e.g. user started
   122→        it manually), we skip launching a new process and just monitor.
   123→        """
   124→        self._running = True
   125→
   126→        # Check if Chrome is already running on the port
   127→        if await self._check_cdp_health():
   128→            logger.info(f'Chrome already running on port {self.debug_port}, skipping launch')
   129→        elif self._config.get('web.chrome_auto_launch', True):
   130→            self._start_chrome_process()
   131→            # Wait for Chrome to be ready
   132→            for _ in range(30):
   133→                if await self._check_cdp_health():
   134→                    logger.info('Chrome CDP endpoint is ready')
   135→                    break
   136→                await asyncio.sleep(1)
   137→            else:
   138→                logger.error('Chrome failed to start within 30 seconds')
   139→        else:
   140→            logger.warning(
   141→                'Chrome auto-launch disabled and no Chrome detected on '
   142→                f'port {self.debug_port}'
   143→            )
   144→
   145→        # Start health check loop
   146→        self._health_task = asyncio.create_task(self._health_loop())
   147→
   148→    async def _health_loop(self) -> None:
   149→        """Periodically check Chrome health and restart if needed."""
   150→        while self._running:
   151→            await asyncio.sleep(10)
   152→            if not self._running:
   153→                break
   154→
   155→            healthy = await self._check_cdp_health()
   156→            if not healthy:
   157→                logger.warning('Chrome health check failed')
   158→                if self._config.get('web.chrome_auto_launch', True):
   159→                    await self._restart_chrome()
   160→
   161→    async def _restart_chrome(self) -> None:
   162→        """Restart Chrome process and reset BrowserManager connection."""
   163→        logger.info('Restarting Chrome...')
   164→
   165→        # Kill existing process if any
   166→        self._kill_process()
   167→
   168→        await asyncio.sleep(2)
   169→
   170→        # Start fresh
   171→        self._start_chrome_process()
   172→
   173→        # Wait for ready
   174→        for _ in range(30):
   175→            if await self._check_cdp_health():
   176→                logger.info('Chrome restarted successfully')
   177→                break
   178→            await asyncio.sleep(1)
   179→        else:
   180→            logger.error('Chrome restart failed')
   181→            return
   182→
   183→        # Reset BrowserManager singleton so it reconnects
   184→        from src.core.browser import BrowserManager
   185→        await BrowserManager.reset_instance()
   186→
   187→    def _kill_process(self) -> None:
   188→        """Terminate the Chrome process."""
   189→        if self._process is None:
   190→            return
   191→
   192→        try:
   193→            self._process.send_signal(signal.SIGTERM)
   194→            try:
   195→                self._process.wait(timeout=5)
   196→            except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   197→                self._process.kill()
   198→                self._process.wait(timeout=5)
   199→            logger.info('Chrome process terminated')
   200→        except Exception as e:
   201→            logger.warning(f'Error terminating Chrome: {e}')
   202→        finally:
   203→            self._process = None
   204→
   205→    async def stop(self) -> None:
   206→        """Stop health monitoring and terminate Chrome."""
   207→        self._running = False
   208→
   209→        if self._health_task and not self._health_task.done():
   210→            self._health_task.cancel()
   211→            try:
   212→                await self._health_task
   213→            except asyncio.CancelledError:
   214→                pass
   215→
   216→        self._kill_process()
   217→        logger.info('ChromeProcessManager stopped')

1→"""Anti-detection module for browser automation.
     2→
     3→Implements human-like interaction via Emunium + Patchright:
     4→- Human-like scrolling behavior (chunked, variable speed)
     5→- Human-like clicking via Emunium (natural mouse movement)
     6→- Human-like typing via Emunium (variable keystroke timing)
     7→
     8→Note: Webdriver property hiding and navigator masking are handled
     9→by Patchright automatically in CDP mode.
    10→"""
    11→
    12→import random
    13→from typing import Optional
    14→
    15→from patchright.async_api import Page
    16→from emunium import EmuniumPlaywright
    17→
    18→from src.exceptions.errors import SelectorNotFoundError
    19→
    20→
    21→async def human_scroll(page: Page, direction: str = 'down', distance: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
    22→    """
    23→    Perform human-like scrolling with variable speed.
    24→
    25→    Args:
    26→        page: Playwright page instance
    27→        direction: 'up' or 'down'
    28→        distance: Scroll distance in pixels (random if None)
    29→    """
    30→    if direction not in ('up', 'down'):
    31→        raise ValueError(f"Invalid scroll direction: {direction!r}, expected 'up' or 'down'")
    32→
    33→    if distance is None:
    34→        distance = random.randint(200, 600)
    35→
    36→    if direction == 'up':
    37→        distance = -distance
    38→
    39→    # Break scroll into smaller chunks
    40→    chunks = random.randint(3, 8)
    41→    chunk_size = distance // chunks
    42→
    43→    for i in range(chunks):
    44→        actual_chunk = chunk_size + random.randint(-20, 20)
    45→        await page.mouse.wheel(0, actual_chunk)
    46→        delay = random.uniform(0.05, 0.15)
    47→        await page.wait_for_timeout(delay * 1000)
    48→
    49→    await page.wait_for_timeout(random.uniform(0.2, 0.5) * 1000)
    50→
    51→
    52→async def human_click(page: Page, selector: str) -> None:
    53→    """
    54→    Click an element with human-like behavior using Emunium.
    55→
    56→    Args:
    57→        page: Playwright page instance
    58→        selector: CSS selector for element to click
    59→    """
    60→    try:
    61→        element = await page.wait_for_selector(selector, timeout=10000)
    62→    except Exception as e:
    63→        if "timeout" in str(type(e).__name__).lower() or "timeout" in str(e).lower():
    64→            raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element not found: {selector}")
    65→        raise
    66→
    67→    if element is None:
    68→        raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element resolved to None: {selector}")
    69→
    70→    emunium = EmuniumPlaywright(page)
    71→    await emunium.click_at(element)
    72→
    73→
    74→async def human_type(page: Page, selector: str, text: str) -> None:
    75→    """
    76→    Type text with human-like timing using Emunium.
    77→
    78→    Args:
    79→        page: Playwright page instance
    80→        selector: CSS selector for input element
    81→        text: Text to type
    82→    """
    83→    try:
    84→        element = await page.wait_for_selector(selector, timeout=10000)
    85→    except Exception as e:
    86→        if "timeout" in str(type(e).__name__).lower() or "timeout" in str(e).lower():
    87→            raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element not found: {selector}")
    88→        raise
    89→
    90→    if element is None:
    91→        raise SelectorNotFoundError(f"Element resolved to None: {selector}")
    92→
    93→    emunium = EmuniumPlaywright(page)

1→"""Session management for persistent browser sessions.
     2→
     3→Handles saving and loading browser sessions (cookies, localStorage)
     4→to enable resuming without re-login.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import json
     8→import os
     9→import stat
    10→import tempfile
    11→from pathlib import Path
    12→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    13→from typing import Optional
    14→
    15→from cryptography.fernet import Fernet
    16→from patchright.async_api import BrowserContext
    17→from loguru import logger
    18→
    19→from src.utils.config import get_config
    20→
    21→
    22→class SessionManager:
    23→    """Manages browser session persistence."""
    24→
    25→    def __init__(self, session_path: Optional[str] = None):
    26→        if session_path is None:
    27→            config = get_config()
    28→            session_path = config.get('data.session_path', 'data/.session/boss_session.json')
    29→
    30→        self.session_path = Path(session_path)
    31→        self._key_path = self.session_path.parent / '.session_key'
    32→        self._fernet: Optional[Fernet] = None
    33→        self._ensure_session_dir()
    34→
    35→    def _ensure_session_dir(self) -> None:
    36→        """Ensure session directory exists with restrictive permissions."""
    37→        session_dir = self.session_path.parent
    38→        session_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    39→        try:
    40→            os.chmod(session_dir, stat.S_IRWXU)
    41→        except OSError:
    42→            logger.warning("Could not set restrictive permissions on session directory")
    43→
    44→    def _get_fernet(self) -> Fernet:
    45→        """Get or create Fernet encryption instance."""
    46→        if self._fernet is not None:
    47→            return self._fernet
    48→
    49→        if self._key_path.exists():
    50→            key = self._key_path.read_bytes().strip()
    51→        else:
    52→            key = Fernet.generate_key()
    53→            fd = os.open(str(self._key_path), os.O_WRONLY | os.O_CREAT | os.O_TRUNC, 0o600)
    54→            try:
    55→                os.write(fd, key)
    56→            finally:
    57→                os.close(fd)
    58→
    59→        self._fernet = Fernet(key)
    60→        return self._fernet
    61→
    62→    def _decrypt_session_data(self) -> dict:
    63→        """Decrypt and parse the stored session file."""
    64→        encrypted = self.session_path.read_bytes()
    65→        plaintext = self._get_fernet().decrypt(encrypted)
    66→        return json.loads(plaintext.decode('utf-8'))
    67→
    68→    def _write_secure(self, path: Path, data: bytes) -> None:
    69→        """Atomically write data to file with owner-only permissions (0600)."""
    70→        tmp_fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=str(path.parent), suffix=".tmp")
    71→        try:
    72→            os.write(tmp_fd, data)
    73→            os.close(tmp_fd)
    74→            tmp_fd = -1
    75→            os.chmod(tmp_path, 0o600)
    76→            os.replace(tmp_path, str(path))
    77→        except BaseException:
    78→            if tmp_fd >= 0:
    79→                os.close(tmp_fd)
    80→            try:
    81→                os.unlink(tmp_path)
    82→            except OSError:
    83→                pass
    84→            raise
    85→
    86→    def has_saved_session(self) -> bool:
    87→        return self.session_path.exists()
    88→
    89→    async def save(self, context: BrowserContext) -> None:
    90→        try:
    91→            storage_state = await context.storage_state()
    92→            session_data = {
    93→                'saved_at': datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
    94→                'storage_state': storage_state
    95→            }
    96→            plaintext = json.dumps(session_data, ensure_ascii=False).encode('utf-8')
    97→            encrypted = self._get_fernet().encrypt(plaintext)
    98→            self._write_secure(self.session_path, encrypted)
    99→            logger.info(f"Session saved (encrypted) to {self.session_path}")
   100→        except Exception as e:
   101→            logger.error(f"Failed to save session: {e}")
   102→            raise
   103→
   104→    async def clear(self) -> None:
   105→        """Clear saved session and encryption key."""
   106→        if self.session_path.exists():
   107→            self.session_path.unlink()
   108→            logger.info("Session cleared")
   109→
   110→        state_path = self.session_path.parent / 'storage_state.json'
   111→        if state_path.exists():
   112→            state_path.unlink()
   113→
   114→        if self._key_path.exists():
   115→            self._key_path.unlink()
   116→        self._fernet = None
   117→
   118→    def get_session_info(self) -> Optional[dict]:
   119→        if not self.has_saved_session():
   120→            return None
   121→
   122→        try:
   123→            session_data = self._decrypt_session_data()
   124→            saved_at = session_data.get('saved_at')
   125→            storage_state = session_data.get('storage_state', {})
   126→            cookies = storage_state.get('cookies', [])
   127→
   128→            if saved_at:
   129→                saved_datetime = datetime.fromisoformat(saved_at)
   130→                if saved_datetime.tzinfo is None:
   131→                    saved_datetime = saved_datetime.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
   132→                age = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - saved_datetime
   133→                age_str = f"{age.days}d {age.seconds // 3600}h"
   134→            else:
   135→                age_str = "unknown"
   136→
   137→            return {
   138→                'saved_at': saved_at,
   139→                'age': age_str,
   140→                'cookie_count': len(cookies),
   141→            }
   142→        except Exception as e:
   143→            logger.error(f"Failed to get session info: {e}")
   144→            return None
   145→
   146→
   147→# Global session manager instance
   148→_session_manager = None
   149→
   150→
   151→def get_session_manager() -> SessionManager:
   152→    """Get global session manager instance."""
   153→    global _session_manager
   154→    if _session_manager is None:
   155→        _session_manager = SessionManager()
   156→    return _session_manager
   157→

1→"""Lightweight database migration system."""
     2→
     3→from typing import Callable, List, Tuple
     4→
     5→from sqlalchemy import text, inspect
     6→from loguru import logger
     7→
     8→_migrations: List[Tuple[int, str, Callable]] = []
     9→
    10→
    11→def migration(version: int, description: str):
    12→    """Decorator to register a migration function."""
    13→    def decorator(fn: Callable):
    14→        _migrations.append((version, description, fn))
    15→        return fn
    16→    return decorator
    17→
    18→
    19→@migration(1, "Initial schema — create core tables if missing")
    20→def _m001_initial_schema(conn):
    21→    """Ensure core tables exist."""
    22→    pass
    23→
    24→
    25→@migration(2, "Add retry_count column to messages table")
    26→def _m002_messages_retry_count(conn):
    27→    inspector = inspect(conn)
    28→    columns = [c["name"] for c in inspector.get_columns("messages")]
    29→    if "retry_count" not in columns:
    30→        conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN retry_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0"))
    31→
    32→
    33→def _ensure_version_table(conn) -> None:
    34→    conn.execute(text("""
    35→        CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
    36→            version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    37→            description TEXT NOT NULL,
    38→            applied_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
    39→        )
    40→    """))
    41→    conn.commit()
    42→
    43→
    44→def _current_version(conn) -> int:
    45→    result = conn.execute(text("SELECT MAX(version) FROM schema_version"))
    46→    row = result.fetchone()
    47→    return row[0] or 0 if row else 0
    48→
    49→
    50→def run_migrations(engine) -> None:
    51→    """Apply all pending migrations."""
    52→    with engine.connect() as conn:
    53→        _ensure_version_table(conn)
    54→        current = _current_version(conn)
    55→
    56→        pending = [(v, d, fn) for v, d, fn in _migrations if v > current]
    57→        if not pending:
    58→            logger.debug(f"Database schema is up-to-date (version {current})")
    59→            return
    60→
    61→        for version, description, fn in sorted(pending):
    62→            logger.info(f"Applying migration v{version}: {description}")
    63→            fn(conn)
    64→            conn.execute(
    65→                text("INSERT INTO schema_version (version, description) VALUES (:v, :d)"),
    66→                {"v": version, "d": description},
    67→            )
    68→            conn.commit()
    69→
    70→        final = _current_version(conn)
    71→        logger.info(f"Migrations complete — schema version {final}")
    72→

Now I have a complete picture of the codebase. Let me compile the full dead code report.

---

## Dead Code Audit Report

### 1. Unused Imports

**`src/web/routes/auth.py` — line 6**
`chrome_manager` is imported but never used in the file body. The `auth_status` and `auth_check` handlers call `get_browser_manager()` directly; `auth_logout` does not touch Chrome at all.
```python
from src.web.app import chrome_manager, templates   # chrome_manager unused
```

---

### 2. Unused Functions / Methods

**`src/utils/validators.py` — `validate_boss_id` (line 9) and `validate_keywords` (line 34)**
Neither function is called from any `src/` file. The only callers are in `tests/test_validators.py`. No production code currently gates on these validators; e.g., the web routes use Pydantic schemas instead.

**`src/utils/delays.py` — `get_random_delay` (line 60)**
Only called by `async_random_delay` internally (line 100). No external file imports or calls it directly. It is an internal helper but is exported at module level with no external callers — borderline, but worth noting.

**`src/core/anti_detection.py` — `human_scroll`, `human_click`, `human_type` (lines 21, 52, 74)**
The entire `anti_detection` module is never imported anywhere in `src/`. Neither production code nor tests reference it. All three public functions are dead from a live-code perspective.
```
grep result: "No matches found" for "from src.core.anti_detection"
```

**`src/utils/logger.py` — `setup_logger` (line 11)**
Only referenced from `scripts/setup_db.py`. Not called anywhere in the `src/` tree, including `run_web.py` or `src/web/app.py`. Loguru is configured inline in each module via `logger.add()`. This function is stranded.

**`src/models/database.py` — `DatabaseManager.drop_all` (lines 181-184)**
Never called from `src/`. Only used in test fixtures (`tests/conftest.py`, `tests/test_database.py`) via `Base.metadata.drop_all(engine)` — not through this method. The method itself has no callers.

**`src/utils/rate_limiter.py` — `reset_rate_limiter` (line 280)**
Defined but never called outside of its own file. No test or production code imports or calls it.

**`src/utils/config.py` — `reset_config` (line 98)**
Defined but never called anywhere in `src/` or tests. Zero callers.

**`src/models/database.py` — `reset_db_manager` (lines 199-202)**
Defined but never called anywhere in `src/` or tests. Zero callers.

**`src/utils/checkpoint.py` — `CheckpointManager.total_results` property (lines 132-133)**
The `total_results` parameter is accepted by `CheckpointManager.save()` (line 44) but `pipeline.py` never passes it (it always uses the default `None`). The `total_results` property reader is consequently never accessed.

---

### 3. Commented-Out Code Blocks

No large blocks (3+ lines) of commented-out code were found in the reviewed files. All comment blocks are docstrings or inline explanations.

---

### 4. Unused Variables / Constants

**`src/utils/checkpoint.py` — `mode` parameter and checkpoint field (lines 45, 61, 141)**
`mode` is accepted as a parameter to `CheckpointManager.save()`, written into the checkpoint JSON (line 61), and read back in `summary` (line 141). However, `pipeline.py` never passes `mode=` (always uses default `"recommend"`), and no caller ever reads `checkpoint.params.get("mode")` for any branching logic. It is stored and displayed but has no functional effect.

**`src/utils/checkpoint.py` — `company` parameter (lines 35, 55)**
`company: tuple` is accepted by `save()` and serialized (`list(company) if company else []`). In `pipeline.py` the only call passes `company=()` (line 380). No part of resume logic reads `checkpoint.params["company"]`. It is a vestigial parameter from a prior design.

**`src/utils/checkpoint.py` — `total_results` parameter (line 44)**
Same issue as the property above: always passed as `None` (default), serialized as `{}`, and the property is never read by live code.

---

### 5. Empty / Trivial Exception Blocks

**`src/models/migrations.py` — `_m001_initial_schema` (lines 21-23)**
Migration v1 is registered with the decorator but the function body is literally `pass`. It performs no work, yet is iterated and committed as a migration version on every fresh install.
```python
@migration(1, "Initial schema — create core tables if missing")
def _m001_initial_schema(conn):
    """Ensure core tables exist."""
    pass
```
This is not a safety issue, but it runs `conn.execute / conn.commit` for a no-op function.

**`src/modules/search.py` — inner `except e: continue` (line 234)**
```python
except (e):
    continue
```
The `except` in the Strategy 2 fallback list extraction silently discards all errors with no logging. It is a true swallowed-exception anti-pattern.

---

### 6. Backwards-Compatibility Shims / Orphaned Artifacts

**`boss_flow_search.py` (root level)**
This is an auto-generated browser recording replay script. It duplicates a `goto` call twice (lines 16-17), uses hardcoded `asyncio.sleep` values (39.4s, etc.), and references selectors that depend on live page state. It is not imported or called by anything in the project; it is a debugging artifact with no production role.

**`src/models/migrations.py` — `retry_count` column migration (lines 25-30)**
Migration v2 adds a `retry_count` column to the `messages` table. However, the `Message` ORM model in `database.py` has no `retry_count` column defined. The column is added to the SQLite table but never mapped or read — the data goes nowhere.

**`src/utils/scoring.py` — `_DEFAULT_WEIGHTS` (lines 78-87)**
This constant is used as the fallback when `settings.json` has no `scoring.weights` key. But `settings.json` already has a fully populated `scoring.weights` block, so `_DEFAULT_WEIGHTS` is never actually used in production. It is purely a safety net / documentation artifact.

---

### Summary Table

| # | File | Lines | Category | Severity |
|---|------|--------|----------|----------|
| 1 | `src/web/routes/auth.py` | 6 | Unused import (`chrome_manager`) | Low |
| 2 | `src/utils/validators.py` | 9, 34 | Unused functions (`validate_boss_id`, `validate_keywords` — no prod callers) | Medium |
| 3 | `src/core/anti_detection.py` | 21, 52, 74 | Entire module unreferenced — all 3 public functions dead | High |
| 4 | `src/utils/logger.py` | 11 | `setup_logger` — not called from `src/` (only `scripts/`) | Low |
| 5 | `src/models/database.py` | 181–184 | `DatabaseManager.drop_all` — no callers | Low |
| 6 | `src/utils/rate_limiter.py` | 280–283 | `reset_rate_limiter` — no callers | Low |
| 7 | `src/utils/config.py` | 98–101 | `reset_config` — no callers | Low |
| 8 | `src/models/database.py` | 199–202 | `reset_db_manager` — no callers | Low |
| 9 | `src/utils/checkpoint.py` | 35, 44–45, 55, 61, 68, 132–133, 141 | `company`, `mode`, `total_results` params/properties — accepted but never meaningfully used | Medium |
| 10 | `src/models/migrations.py` | 19–23 | Migration v1 `pass` body — no-op registered migration | Low |
| 11 | `src/modules/search.py` | 234 | Bare `except: continue` swallowing errors silently | Medium |
| 12 | `boss_flow_search.py` | entire file | Recording replay artifact — not imported, duplicate gotos, hardcoded sleeps | Medium |
| 13 | `src/models/migrations.py` | 25–30 | `retry_count` column added but ORM model has no such field — orphaned migration | Medium |

### Priority Recommendations

1. **Delete `src/core/anti_detection.py`** — 100% unreferenced. If Emunium-based interaction is needed later, it should be reintroduced intentionally.
2. **Delete or repurpose `boss_flow_search.py`** — pure debug artifact in the repo root.
3. **Fix migration v2 / `retry_count` mismatch** — either add `retry_count` to the `Message` ORM model or drop the migration. Currently the column exists in SQLite but is invisible to SQLAlchemy.
4. **Remove unused `company`, `mode`, `total_results` from `CheckpointManager.save()`** — the API surface is misleading since pipeline.py never uses them.
5. **Remove `validate_boss_id` and `validate_keywords`** — or wire them into the web/CLI layers where they would actually provide value.
6. **Remove the 5 reset/helper functions** (`reset_rate_limiter`, `reset_config`, `reset_db_manager`) and `DatabaseManager.drop_all` if they will not be used in tests — currently they sit as dead surface area.
7. **Fix the silent `except: continue`** at `src/modules/search.py:234` — at minimum add `logger.debug`.
